suggestion: The topic scores well — no pivot needed. To strengthen novelty for a top venue (AAMAS/AAAI), sharpen the contribution around the problem class rather than the specific game instance. Two refinements: (1) Frame as 'Distributed Constraint Satisfaction under Adversarial Partial Observability' — this positions the Scale Game as a concrete instance of a general class that includes sensor fusion with untrusted sources, Byzantine-tolerant distributed inference, and multi-agent scientific experimentation. The game-show framing hooks readers; the abstraction satisfies reviewers. (2) The strongest publishable result is likely the equilibrium characterization (SQ3): proving a clean threshold condition where truthful reporting transitions from equilibrium to dominated strategy as competition intensity increases. This is analytically tractable for the 7-player instance and would be a genuine contribution to mechanism design / information design literature. The RL experiments are supporting evidence, not the headline. Risk to watch: if the information-theoretic bounds (SQ2) turn out to be a trivial constant factor under honest play, lead with the strategic layer instead.
